feedback.text: I strongly believe that players should retain access to the games theyve legally purchased, even after official server support ends. In the case of The Crew, the game wasnt just made unplayable, it was removed entirely from existence. A title I paid for, enjoyed, and wanted to revisit was wiped out because the publisher chose to shut it down. Thats not just frustrating, its a loss of digital culture and consumer rights. The petitions call for publishers to offer offline modes or allow private/community servers when a game reaches end-of-life is not only fair, its necessary. Right now, theres no protection for players when a game is taken down completely. What happened with The Crew shows how fragile digital "ownership" really is. We need rules that ensure games can live on beyond corporate shutdowns, for both players and preservation. Thank you and have a nice day.
